question.text: Spot price of copper is Rs 750 per kg. The 3-month futures are trading at Rs 770. If the 3-month cost-of-carry is Rs 15, what is the basis?

answer.text: Rs -20 (spot is below futures by Rs 20)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Basis = Spot - Futures = 750 - 770 = -Rs 20. A negative basis indicates contango (futures above spot). The cost-of-carry fair value would be 765, so futures are slightly overpriced. **The Trap:** Rs 20 ignores the sign (basis convention is spot minus futures). Rs 5 uses carry minus premium. Basis is always Spot - Futures. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers basis risk in equity derivatives hedging. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Widening negative basis (deeper contango) may signal arbitrage opportunity — buy spot, sell futures. Narrowing basis towards expiry is the normal convergence pattern.
